findings.message: Documented policy instructs developers to disguise security-patching releases as bland refactors and strip security-related keywords from public changelog, commit, and release notes. Prevents downstream users from identifying security-relevant updates.

Release checklist contains three instances of the literal string <personal-or-internal-string> with no explanation that substitution is required or what to substitute, making the checklist non-actionable for new contributors.

npm build lifecycle executes `rm -rf dist` as a shell command. Scoped to dist/ in practice, but a symlink or directory confusion at that path would cause unintended deletion; also runs on every npm publish via prepublishOnly.
All four runtime dependencies and all dev dependencies use ^ semver ranges. A malicious or broken minor release in any dependency could silently affect downstream installs.
skills/SKILL.md advertises '65 tools' but CLAUDE.md:14 states 'registers all 63 canonical tools'. One count is stale after a recent tool addition.

The metadata: frontmatter block embeds JSON flow syntax ({ "openclaw": { ... } }) inside a YAML document. Valid but non-standard; strict-mode YAML parsers and frontmatter linters commonly reject inline JSON flow mappings at the top level.

Tool count in skills/SKILL.md ('65 tools') disagrees with CLAUDE.md ('63 canonical tools'). CLI version option tables in SKILL.md are frozen at 2.1.111/2.1.121 while CLAUDE.md notes tested version 2.1.178.

SKILL.md contains relative Markdown links to references/council.md, references/autoloop.md, and references/tools.md that could not be verified during this audit. If absent from the repo, these are broken references for readers in editor or on GitHub.

Behavioral contract uses two vague quantifiers in one sentence: 'briefly' (no length bound) and '(when relevant)' (no inclusion criteria). Agents following this instruction have undefined behavior on length and conditional inclusion.

File is registered as a skill reference (skills/ultraapp/SKILL.md) but is structured as an agent persona: imperative behavioral contracts, tool invocation syntax, and a completion protocol. Content role does not match file-system classification.
skills/SKILL.md advertises '65 tools' but CLAUDE.md:14 states 'registers all 63 canonical tools'. One count is stale after a recent tool addition.

Documented policy instructs developers to disguise security-patching releases as bland refactors and strip security-related keywords from public changelog, commit, and release notes. Prevents downstream users from identifying security-relevant updates. — Lines 113-115 read: 'must NOT name what was leaked ... include words like "security" / "hard-coded" / "sanitize". Frame as a bland refactor.'

npm build lifecycle executes `rm -rf dist` as a shell command. Scoped to dist/ in practice, but a symlink or directory confusion at that path would cause unintended deletion; also runs on every npm publish via prepublishOnly. — "build": "rm -rf dist && tsc && node scripts/postbuild.mjs" — rm -rf executed unconditionally in npm build and via prepublishOnly.

All four runtime dependencies and all dev dependencies use ^ semver ranges. A malicious or broken minor release in any dependency could silently affect downstream installs. — All runtime deps use ^ ranges: "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk": "^1.29.0", "commander": "^12.1.0", "diff": "^9.0.0", "re2": "^1.24.1"

Release checklist contains three instances of the literal string <personal-or-internal-string> with no explanation that substitution is required or what to substitute, making the checklist non-actionable for new contributors.

The metadata: frontmatter block embeds JSON flow syntax ({ "openclaw": { ... } }) inside a YAML document. Valid but non-standard; strict-mode YAML parsers and frontmatter linters commonly reject inline JSON flow mappings at the top level.

Behavioral contract uses two vague quantifiers in one sentence: 'briefly' (no length bound) and '(when relevant)' (no inclusion criteria). Agents following this instruction have undefined behavior on length and conditional inclusion. — Line 17: 'In the `context` field, briefly explain why you're asking this and (when relevant) what you observed'

File is registered as a skill reference (skills/ultraapp/SKILL.md) but is structured as an agent persona: imperative behavioral contracts, tool invocation syntax, and a completion protocol. Content role does not match file-system classification.

Tool count in skills/SKILL.md ('65 tools') disagrees with CLAUDE.md ('63 canonical tools'). CLI version option tables in SKILL.md are frozen at 2.1.111/2.1.121 while CLAUDE.md notes tested version 2.1.178.

SKILL.md contains relative Markdown links to references/council.md, references/autoloop.md, and references/tools.md that could not be verified during this audit. If absent from the repo, these are broken references for readers in editor or on GitHub.
